name: Pisnyi Borsch (Meatless Beet Soup)
recipeIngredient: 3 Dried Boletus Mushrooms, OR 1/2 lb Chopped Mushrooms
Liquid From The Cooked 1 quart Beet Kvas, OR 1 t (tablespoon) Salt
1 t Ground Pepper, Or To Taste
recipeInstructions:
Soak boletus mushrooms overnight. Cook in a little water until tender. Cool and reserve the liquid, then chop finely.
Scrub beets and cut into quarters. Cover with water and cook over low heat until tender (about 1 to 2 hours). Cool and pour off the liquid, reserving it. Slip off the peels. Wear rubber gloves to prevent purple hands. This can be done a day in advance.
Peel and cut up the other vegetables. Add the bay leaf, peppercorns, boletus or mushrooms to the vegetables with enough water to cover, and cook in a large aluminum pot over low heat until tender. Strain the beet liquid into the vegetables.
Shred the beets in a processor or on a medium grater, then add. Simmer for about 10 minutes and strain into a large pot. Do not press the vegetables to keep the broth clear. Add the beet kvas, mushroom liquid, pepper, salt, and bring to a gentle boil. Turn off the heat.
Taste; the flavor should be tart, mellow, and full. For more tartness, add fresh lemon juice or sour salt. Keeps well in the refrigerator. Reheat gently; do not overcook as this will change the color. To serve, pour over 3 or 4 vushka (dumplings) in soup plates and garnish with fresh dill.
